chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1188 of 2016 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For directions For hearing of CMA No.704/2020 (C/A) 27-10-2022 Mr. Daim Hussain Bhanbhro, Advocate holding brief for Mr. Mehfooz Ahmed Awan, Advocate for the petitioner / applicant. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- By means of listed application, the petitioner / applicant seeks initiation of contempt proceedings against alleged contemnors No.1 & 2 on the ground that they deliberately, intentionally and willfully have flouted / violated the lawful order dated 12-04-2016, whereby this petition was disposed of with directions to the respondents to consider the application of the petitioner for his appointment on deceased quota strictly in accordance with law, rules and requisite notification within a period of three months. In response, the statement has been filed on behalf of Secretary, Local Government Department, Government of Sindh (respondent / alleged contemnor No.1) stating therein that the deceased quota policy is applicable to the sons / daughters of the deceased Government employees who expired on or after 02-09-2002. Prior to said date, no such policy was existed. The petitioner’s father was expired on 20-06-2001; hence, he is not eligible for his appointment against deceased quota. A copy of the decision made by the respondents is annexed with the statement as ‘Annexure-III’, which reflects that the decision on the application of the petitioner was passed on 08-12-2020; hence, the compliance of order dated 12-04-2016 was made by the respondents Accordingly, this application being devoid of merits is dismissed.

The petitioner’s father was expired on 20-06-2001; hence, he is not eligible for his appointment against deceased quota. A copy of the decision made by the respondents is annexed with the statement as ‘Annexure-III’, which reflects that the decision on the application of the petitioner was passed on 08-12-2020; hence, the compliance of order dated 12-04-2016 was made by the respondents Accordingly, this application being devoid of merits is dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
